event: treated with aspirin (enteric coated tab, 100 mg)
Aspirin was stopped 7 d before surgery
no medication was administered on the morning of surgery
Echocardiogram showed a left ventricular ejection fraction of 61%
Bupivacaine (0.5%, 10 mg) was injected into the subarachnoid space at T10 for the spinal block
Intravenous dexmedetomidine was infused (0.5 μg/kg/min for first 10 min, then at 0.4-0.6 μg/kg/min) for sedation
A pneumatic thigh tourniquet was applied intraoperatively at an inflation pressure of 300 mmHg for 100 min
Total anesthesia time was 2 h and 10 min
During surgery, 600 mL of crystalloid was administered
150 mL of urine were collected during surgery
Immediately after surgery laboratory results were within the normal range
About 90 mL of urine were drained
post-operative pain managed with an intravenous patient-controlled analgesia including 1 mg fentanyl
Antibiotics (Refosporen, 1 g and cefazedone sodium 1 g) were injected bid
severe pain on the right thigh
Tridol Injection (tramadol hydrochloride) was injected for pain control
urine output 100 mL (0-8 h after operation)
urine output 220 mL (8-16 h after operation)
urine output 250 mL (16-24 h after operation)
19 h after surgery creatine kinase-myocardial band 13.26 ng/mL
right thigh was stiff and had turned a dark brown, without swelling
Urine collected in a Foley bag was dark colored
red blood cell count in urine was 100/HPF
Arterial line was placed in the left radial artery
central line was secured in the right internal jugular vein
fluid treatment of 5 L/d was prescribed
Fluid loading was initiated with normal saline at 400 mL/h for 2 h, then decreased to 250 mL/h
urine output 110 mL for the first 3 h
urine output 600 mL for next 4 h
mental status was restored to alert
